output: Batch 1 complete. T1 reduced `PinnedShotGroup` to soft-tag shape in `src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts` (removed `start`/`children`, kept `shotId`/`trackId`/`clipIds`/`mode?`/`videoAssetKey?`/`imageClipSnapshot?`, added ordering-comment). Expected `.start`/`.children` TS errors now appear in downstream consumers — these are scheduled for T2–T11. T4 moved `interactionStateRef` ownership from `TimelineEditor.tsx` into `useTimelineState.ts` as a single shared `useRef`, threaded it into `useTimelineSave` → `useTimelinePersistence`/`usePollSync` via explicit hook arguments, and exposed it through `TimelineEditorDataContextValue` so `TimelineEditor` reads the same instance from context and still forwards it to `useClipDrag` and `TimelineCanvas`. `VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx` and `usePollSync.test.ts` pass with the new plumbing. Persistence/poll accept the ref but don't yet read it (T14 wires the gating). Broader test suite intentionally not run in this batch since many tests assert on fields removed in T1 and will be updated in T15.
deviations: Persistence and poll hooks accept the new `interactionStateRef` argument but currently `void` it; actual gating is deferred to T14 per plan. A brief code comment states this. This keeps T4 scoped to plumbing as the task description requires.

Did not run the full video-editor vitest suite: T1 intentionally removes PinnedShotGroup fields that dozens of existing tests still assert on, so the suite will produce cascading failures that are explicitly scheduled for T15. Ran only tests relevant to T4's plumbing (usePollSync, VideoEditorProvider) — both pass.

task_updates.executor_notes: Reduced PinnedShotGroup in src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts to soft-tag fields only: shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode?, videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot?. Removed start, children, and the prior invariants JSDoc. Added a one-line comment above the type documenting that clipIds is ordered left-to-right by each clip's live `at` and rebuilt whenever membership/ordering changes. Downstream TS errors on .start/.children are expected and will be addressed by T2-T11 per plan.

Ownership of interactionStateRef moved from TimelineEditor.tsx:191 local useRef into useTimelineState.ts (single shared ref created via useRef at the top of the hook). Threaded into useTimelineSave as a new required third argument; useTimelineSave forwards it to useTimelinePersistence and usePollSync (both options interfaces extended). Exposed on TimelineEditorDataContextValue via useTimelineState.types.ts and useTimelineState.contexts.ts; VideoEditorProvider's editorData memo now includes interactionStateRef. TimelineEditor reads interactionStateRef from useTimelineEditorData() and forwards the same ref object to useClipDrag (prop) and TimelineCanvas (prop); isInteractionActive closure depends on the context ref. useClipDrag write sites (drag start/end) and TimelineCanvas resize write sites (resize start/end) now observe the single shared ref. Persistence and poll currently `void` the ref — T14 will wire the actual gate. Verified with usePollSync.test.ts (pass) and VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx (pass). No new TS errors introduced by plumbing beyond the pre-existing baseline and the T1-induced PinnedShotGroup cascade.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. PinnedShotGroup now contains only { shotId, trackId, clipIds, mode?, videoAssetKey?, imageClipSnapshot? }. start and children (including their prior JSDoc invariants block) are removed. A one-line comment above the type documents that clipIds is ordered left-to-right by each clip's live `at` and rebuilt whenever group membership or ordering changes.

Confirmed. interactionStateRef is created once via useRef in useTimelineState.ts (before useTimelineSave is called) and is the single instance threaded into useTimelineSave (as a new required arg), then forwarded by useTimelineSave to useTimelinePersistence and usePollSync (both options extended with `interactionStateRef: MutableRefObject<{drag,resize}>`). The same ref is exposed via TimelineEditorDataContextValue and consumed by TimelineEditor, which forwards it unchanged to useClipDrag (drag writers) and TimelineCanvas (resize writers). All five consumers observe the same ref instance.
